meaning: n.(登山运动中的)沿绳滑下法
examples: Beghin had just began to abseil when the artificial anchor popped out.
If you do find yourself on a route that is too long to lower off, the easiest way is to pull a spare rope up and abseil off.
If staying in a TV tower isn't crazy enough, you can also arrange to abseil its 100m height, an unusual proposition as there's no wall to rest your feet against.
The numerous mountains offer many options to serious climbers, but anyone with an adrenaline craving can take the Abseil Africa challenge.
Engineers had to abseil about 80ft (25m) down a cliff to examine a Devon rail line which was closed after a landslip.
